meta.project: Magic Agents Operator Workspace UI port

stories.title: Scaffold the React + TypeScript + Vite frontend
stories.notes: The ui/ React + TypeScript + Vite workspace now builds reproducibly with npm, serves a compiled bundle through FastAPI, and passes the frontend foundation backend contract tests.

stories.title: Establish a typed API client and query layer
stories.notes: Typed request helpers, workspace API contracts, SSE support, and TanStack Query hooks are in place and covered by Vitest plus backend foundation coverage.

stories.title: Build the application shell and provider stack
stories.notes: AppProviders, ThemeProvider, router wiring, AppErrorBoundary, NotFoundView, and the reusable AppShell now support a routed operator workspace with tested shell states.

stories.title: Establish the tokenized visual system and typography rules
stories.notes: Central CSS tokens and base styles now drive color, spacing, typography, radius, and motion, with token enforcement verified in the shared style tests.

stories.title: Build accessible UI primitives for the workspace
stories.notes: Buttons, badges, text primitives, rails, sections, artifact panels, empty states, and skeletons are shared across the shell and covered by component tests.

stories.title: Implement the three-pane workspace layout
stories.notes: The shell now renders a center-dominant three-pane workspace on desktop with independent pane scroll regions and tested landmarks.

stories.title: Add the sticky phase rail and session status strip
stories.notes: A sticky seven-phase rail and persistent session status strip stay visible inside the center workspace while content scrolls.

stories.title: Replace screen jumps with panel state transitions
stories.notes: Center and inspector surfaces now swap in place with React-owned transitions and focus handoff instead of full-screen route changes.

stories.title: Add a top bar with session context and workspace controls
stories.notes: The top bar now exposes product identity, current story context, current phase summary, session status, and pane controls in one stable strip.

stories.title: Support panel collapse, persistence, and responsive fallbacks
stories.notes: Operators can collapse either side pane with persisted preferences, and compact widths switch to overlay panels while restoring the desktop layout predictably.

stories.title: Unify Jira intake and manual entry in the left rail
Start, resume, and recover sessions from checkpoints
Model session state in React without UI drift
Render a phase-aware acceptance criteria checklist
Display a left-rail phase progress timeline
Surface session health and usage telemetry
